pull_request.title: BUG: stats.levy_stable: fix PDF in Tails for `beta=+-1`
pull_request.body: #### Reference issue Closes #20636 by fixing integration. #### What does this implement/fix? The PDF of the Levy Stable distribution did not produce the correct results in the far tails for $\beta = \pm 1$. This PR fixes that as show in the graph where the y-axis is the log of the pdf Furthermore, for the S1 parameterization with $\alpha\approx 1$ and $\beta=\pm 1$, the PDF was wildly off as shown in the graph where the y-axis is the log of the pdf With this PR, the above graph looks like #### Additional information Correcting these issues was accomplished with two main fixes. First, I modified the points used to integrate in the function `_pdf_single_value_piecewise_post_rounding_Z0`. The integrand in this function goes from very peaked for small $x$ to somewhat broad for large $x$. The previous method to get the integration points worked for an integrand that was peaked but not broad. To fix this, I caught when the previous technique broke down and then chose evenly spaced integration points which works well for a broad integrand. For very small $x$, the integrand becomes so peaked that integration is hopeless. To fix this, I used a Taylor Series for small $x$ which fixes the "step" behavior we see in the second graph above. One issue is that this implementation is about 2-3x slower than the previous implementation. This could probably be fixed by vectorizing the function `g` in [`levyst.c`](https://github.com/scipy/scipy/blob/main/scipy/stats/_levy_stable/c_src/levyst.c), but I figured I'd wait for a review before trying that.

changed_files.patch:
@@ -281,7 +281,14 @@ def integrand(theta):
             for exp_height in [100, 10, 5]
             # exp_height = 1 is handled by peak
         ]
-        intg_points = [0, peak] + tail_points
+        # Symmetry is used to ensure x > zeta as above. Integration 
+        # becomes numerically unstable so let `quad` handle 
+        # integration points
+        if beta != -1:
+            intg_points = [0, peak] + tail_points
+        else:
+            intg_points = None
+
         intg, *ret = integrate.quad(
             integrand,
             -xi,

@@ -6400,6 +6400,26 @@ def test_frozen_parameterization_gh20821b(self, levy_stable_lock):
             frozen_b = frozen.rvs(size=10, random_state=rng(329823498))
             assert_equal(frozen_b, unfrozen_b)
 
+    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
+        "param, alpha, beta, xs, expected",
+        [
+            (0, 1.6, 1, [-15, -14, -13], [-187.1444263, -157.1411663, -130.3526688]),
+            (1, 1.6, 1, [-15, -14, -13], [-165.0203129, -137.3671717, -112.8223545]),
+            (0, 1.4, -1, [13, 14, 15], [-367.1943293, -464.7049565, -579.4399286]),
+            (1, 1.4, -1, [13, 14, 15], [-258.1216497, -334.6139857, -426.0724094]),
+        ],
+    )
+    @pytest.mark.xfail_on_32bit("`bisect` fails to recognize valid bracket")
+    def test_logpdf_tail(self, param, alpha, beta, xs, expected):
+        # gh-20636
+        # Expected values computed from Mathematica. Mathematica command is:
+        # Log[PDF[StableDistribution[0, 1.6, 1, 0, 1], -15.0]] // InputForm
+        # Test levy_stable.logpdf accuracy in the tails when beta=1 or beta=-1
+        # for both S0/S1 parameterizations.
+        # Testing logpdf instead of pdf as more compact.
+        stats.levy_stable.parameterization = f"S{param}"
+        logpdfs = stats.levy_stable(alpha=alpha, beta=beta).logpdf(xs)
+        assert_allclose(logpdfs, expected, rtol=1e-5)
 
 class TestArrayArgument:  # test for ticket:992
     def setup_method(self):

commit_summaries.message: Fixed precision issues in tail of levy_stable distribution [skip ci]
Failing for S1 with alpha~1 and x~0
Fixed small x precision issues; had to increase pdf x tolerance
Catch ValueError not all exceptions [skip ci]
Reverted changes to main branch [skip ci]
